steps.title: Translate the two facts into equations
steps.content:
$x$ = length, $y$ = height.
Fact 1: Height is 61 inches → $y=61$.
Fact 2: Length is 3 times height → $x=3y$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y=3x$, $x=61$': Sets length to 61 (should be height) and says height = 3 × length (reversed). ✗
  • Choice B — '$y=3x$, $y=61$': Correctly sets $y=61$ but reverses the relationship: says height = 3 × length instead of length = 3 × height. ✗
  • Choice C — '$x=3y$, $x=61$': Correct relationship $x=3y$, but assigns 61 to length $x$ instead of height $y$. ✗
  • Choice D — '$x=3y$, $y=61$': $x=3y$ correctly states length = 3 × height; $y=61$ correctly sets height to 61 inches. That matches choice D — D is correct. ✓
